title: 9 Herbal Essential Oils For Benign Prostatic Hypertrophy
intro_desc: If you're dealing with benign prostatic hypertrophy (BPH), you know how uncomfortable and painful it can be. One natural way to help alleviate symptoms is by using herbal essential oils. These oils are extracted from plants that have been used for centuries to support men's health. For example, Serenoa repens, also known as saw palmetto, has been shown to block the conversion of testosterone to dihydrotestosterone (DHT), a hormone that contributes to BPH.  Urtica dioica, or stinging nettle, is another herb that may help. Its root and leaves contain compounds that reduce inflammation and promote the health of the prostate gland. Curcuma longa, or turmeric, is also worth mentioning. Curcumin, a compound found in turmeric, has potent anti-inflammatory properties that may help ease BPH symptoms.  When you use these herbal essential oils in the form of teas or supplements, they can bring several benefits to your life. They may help you urinate more easily, reduce the size of an enlarged prostate, and even alleviate symptoms like painful urination or frequent trips to the bathroom.  These herbal teas can also help you maintain a healthy prostate and support your overall well-being. Some people find that they improve their quality of life, allowing them to engage in activities they love without being held back by BPH symptoms.
intro_image_alt: herbal essential oils for benign prostatic hypertrophy

remedies.remedy_desc: Serenoa repens essential oils contains active constituents such as saw palmetto and phytosterols, which have been identified as key components that help alleviate symptoms of benign prostatic hypertrophy (BPH). Phytosterols, particularly beta-sitosterol, have been shown to inhibit the conversion of testosterone to dihydrotestosterone (DHT), a hormone that contributes to prostate growth. Saw palmetto's ability to block DHT receptors helps reduce prostate size and alleviate urinary symptoms associated with BPH. The anti-inflammatory properties of Serenoa repens essential oils may also help reduce swelling and discomfort in the prostate area. By inhibiting DHT and reducing inflammation, Serenoa repens essential oils may provide relief from BPH symptoms.
remedies.image_alt: Serenoa repens herbal essential oils for benign prostatic hypertrophy
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of Serenoa repens leaves, dried and crushed.
Add 1 cup of carrier oil (like coconut oil) to the crushed leaves in a clean glass jar.
Steep the mixture in a cool, dark place for 2-3 weeks, shaking the jar daily.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass jar.
Discard the solids and store the Serenoa repens essential oil in a dark glass bottle with a tight-fitting lid.

remedies.remedy_desc: Urtica dioica essential oils contains a compound called boivin, which is known to help with benign prostatic hypertrophy. The oil also contains other active constituents like sesquiterpenes, which have anti-inflammatory properties that can reduce swelling in the prostate gland. The diuretic properties of Urtica dioica essential oil help to reduce water retention and alleviate urinary retention, a common symptom of benign prostatic hypertrophy. The oil's ability to relax the muscles in the urinary tract also helps to improve urine flow, making it easier to pass urine. By reducing inflammation and improving urinary function, Urtica dioica essential oil may help alleviate symptoms of benign prostatic hypertrophy.
remedies.image_alt: Urtica dioica herbal essential oils for benign prostatic hypertrophy
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh Urtica dioica leaves and flowers, and 1 cup of carrier oil (such as jojoba or grapeseed oil)
Wash the Urtica dioica leaves and flowers with cold water, and gently pat them dry with a paper towel
Combine the Urtica dioica leaves and flowers with 1 cup of carrier oil in a clean glass jar, making sure they are completely covered
Let the mixture sit in a cool, dark place for 2-3 weeks, shaking the jar daily to help the oil infuse into the plant material
After 2-3 weeks, strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass bottle, and discard the solids

remedies.remedy_desc: Curcuma longa essential oils contains a compound called curcumin, which is a powerful antioxidant and anti-inflammatory. Curcumin has been shown to inhibit the growth of prostate cells, reducing the size of the prostate gland. The bioactive constituents in Curcuma longa essential oils, including curcumin, turmeric oil, and beta-carophyllene, have been found to reduce inflammation in the prostate, a key contributor to benign prostatic hypertrophy (BPH). These properties help to alleviate symptoms of BPH, such as difficulty urinating and frequent urination. By reducing inflammation and inhibiting prostate cell growth, Curcuma longa essential oils may help to alleviate the symptoms of BPH.
remedies.image_alt: Curcuma longa herbal essential oils for benign prostatic hypertrophy
remedy_procedure: Gather 50g of Curcuma longa roots, 1 cup of carrier oil (such as coconut or olive oil), and a clean glass container.
Dry the Curcuma longa roots in a low-temperature oven (150°F) for 2 hours, then let them cool.
Grind the dried roots into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle.
Mix 10g of the powdered roots with 1 cup of carrier oil in the glass container, then seal it and let it sit in a cool, dark place for 2 weeks, shaking the container daily.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into another clean glass container, discard the solids, and store the essential oil in a dark glass bottle.

remedies.remedy_desc: Ginkgo biloba essential oils contains the bioactive constituents flavonoids and bilobalide, which have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. These properties help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the prostate, which can contribute to benign prostatic hypertrophy (BPH). The flavonoids in Ginkgo biloba have been shown to inhibit the growth of prostate tissue and reduce the production of prostate-specific antigen (PSA). Bilobalide has been found to have a relaxing effect on smooth muscle, which can help reduce the size and symptoms of an enlarged prostate. Regular use of Ginkgo biloba essential oils may help alleviate symptoms of BPH by reducing inflammation and promoting relaxation in the prostate tissue.
remedies.image_alt: Ginkgo biloba herbal essential oils for benign prostatic hypertrophy
remedy_procedure: Gather 500g of Ginkgo biloba leaves and 1 cup of carrier oil (such as coconut or jojoba oil).
Chop the Ginkgo biloba leaves into small pieces and place them in a clean glass jar.
Add 1 cup of carrier oil to the jar and stir well. Let it sit in a cool, dark place for 2-3 weeks, shaking the jar daily.
After 2-3 weeks, strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass jar. Discard the solids.
Store the Ginkgo biloba essential oil in a dark glass bottle with a tight-fitting lid and label it. Use 5-7 drops per application to help alleviate benign prostatic hypertrophy symptoms.

remedies.remedy_desc: Zingiber officinale essential oils contains bioactive constituents like gingerol and shogaol, which have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. These properties help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the prostate gland, a common factor in benign prostatic hypertrophy. The oils' ability to inhibit prostaglandin synthesis also contributes to its potential benefits, as prostaglandins play a role in promoting prostate enlargement. Additionally, the oils' antioxidant properties help protect the prostate gland from cell damage and promote healthy cell growth. By reducing inflammation and promoting healthy cell growth, Zingiber officinale essential oils may help alleviate symptoms associated with benign prostatic hypertrophy.
remedies.image_alt: Zingiber officinale herbal essential oils for benign prostatic hypertrophy
remedy_procedure: Gather 2 cups of fresh Zingiber officinale (Ginger) roots and clean them with water.
Cut the ginger roots into small pieces and put them in a clean glass jar.
Add 1 cup of carrier oil (like coconut or olive oil) to the jar and close it.
Leave the jar in a cool, dark place for 2-3 weeks, shaking it every day to help the oil extract from the ginger.
After 2-3 weeks, strain the oil mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass jar. Discard the solids and store the oil in a cool, dark place.

remedies.remedy_desc: Silybum marianum essential oils contains bioactive constituents such as silymarin, flavonoids, and terpenoids. These compounds have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help reduce swelling and damage to the prostate gland. The flavonoids in Silybum marianum essential oils, specifically kaempferol and quercetin, have been shown to inhibit the growth of prostate cells and reduce the production of prostate-specific antigen. The terpenoids, including silibinin, have been found to have anti-androgenic effects, which can help block the production of male hormones that contribute to prostate enlargement. By reducing inflammation, inhibiting cell growth, and blocking hormone production, Silybum marianum essential oils may provide relief from the symptoms of benign prostatic hypertrophy.
remedies.image_alt: Silybum marianum herbal essential oils for benign prostatic hypertrophy
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of dried Silybum marianum flowers and leaves. You can buy them from a store or grow your own.
Use a coffee grinder or spice grinder to grind 1/4 cup of the dried flowers and leaves into a fine powder.
Transfer the powder to a glass jar with a lid. Pour 2 cups of carrier oil, such as coconut or olive oil, over the powder.
Close the jar and let it sit in a cool, dark place for 2-3 weeks, shaking the jar every day. This is called cold infusion.
After 2-3 weeks, strain the oil through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into another glass jar. Discard the solids and store the oil in a cool, dark place.

remedies.remedy_desc: Rosmarinus officinalis essential oils contains rosmarinic acid, camphor, and borneol as its bioactive constituents. These compounds have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that can help reduce the size of an enlarged prostate. The anti-inflammatory effects of rosmarinic acid may help decrease swelling and pain associated with benign prostatic hypertrophy. Borneol's analgesic and antispasmodic properties may also help alleviate urinary frequency and urgency caused by an enlarged prostate. By reducing inflammation and promoting relaxation, Rosmarinus officinalis essential oils may provide relief from symptoms of benign prostatic hypertrophy.
remedies.image_alt: Rosmarinus officinalis herbal essential oils for benign prostatic hypertrophy
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh Rosmarinus officinalis leaves, 1 cup of water, and a glass jar with a lid.
Wash the leaves in cold water, then gently pat them dry with a paper towel.
Combine the leaves and water in the glass jar, making sure the leaves are fully submerged.
Steep the mixture in a warm, dark place for 2-3 weeks, shaking the jar daily to release the oils.
Strain the liquid into another container, discarding the solids. This is your Rosmarinus officinalis essential oil.

remedies.remedy_desc: Avena sativa essential oils contains a compound called avenanthramides, which are responsible for its benefits. These avenanthramides have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce swelling and pain in the prostate. Avena sativa essential oils also contains a compound called beta-sitosterol, a natural anti-androgen that can help block the conversion of testosterone into dihydrotestosterone (DHT), a hormone that contributes to the enlargement of the prostate. The diuretic properties of Avena sativa essential oils can also help increase urine flow and reduce the pressure on the prostate. The antioxidant properties of avenanthramides in Avena sativa essential oils can help protect the prostate from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals.
remedies.image_alt: Avena sativa herbal essential oils for benign prostatic hypertrophy
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of dried Avena sativa (oat straw) and 2 cups of carrier oil (like jojoba or sweet almond oil).
Add 1/4 cup of dried Avena sativa to a clean glass jar and cover with 1 cup of carrier oil.
Steep the mixture in a cool, dark place for 2-3 weeks, shaking the jar every day.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into another clean glass jar, discarding the solids.
Store the Avena sativa essential oil in a dark glass bottle, labeling it with the date and ingredients.

remedies.remedy_desc: Piper nigrum essential oils contains a compound called piperine, which is known to have anti-inflammatory properties. This helps to reduce swelling and discomfort in the prostate gland. The essential oil also contains beta-caryophyllene, a sesquiterpene with anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help to alleviate symptoms of benign prostatic hypertrophy. Piper nigrum essential oils has been found to inhibit the growth of prostate cancer cells and reduce the size of the prostate gland. The anti-inflammatory compounds in Piper nigrum essential oils can help to reduce the risk of prostate-related complications.
remedies.image_alt: Piper nigrum herbal essential oils for benign prostatic hypertrophy
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of Piper nigrum seeds and 2 cups of carrier oil (such as coconut or olive oil).
Heat 1 cup of water in a saucepan and add the Piper nigrum seeds. Let it simmer for 10-15 minutes.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a bowl. Discard the seeds.
Add the strained mixture to the carrier oil and stir well. Let it sit for 2-3 hours or overnight.
Strain the mixture again through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean container. The essential oil is now ready for use.

faq_prevent: Drinking herbal tea may help prevent benign prostatic hypertrophy from forming. Some herbal teas, like saw palmetto and stinging nettle, contain compounds that may block hormones that can cause the prostate to enlarge. These teas may also reduce inflammation and improve urine flow, which can help keep the prostate healthy.
faq_safe: While herbal teas like saw palmetto and nettle root may help with benign prostatic hypertrophy symptoms, consuming them daily can be safe for some people. However, individual results vary, and it's essential to note that potential interactions with medications or underlying health conditions exist. Monitor your body's response and adjust your consumption accordingly.
faq_timeline: The results of herbal teas for benign prostatic hypertrophy can vary, but some people experience relief within a few weeks to a few months. Saw palmetto and stinging nettle are two common herbs that may help reduce symptoms like frequent urination and enlargement. Improvement can be gradual, so patience is key.
faq_time: Drinking herbal tea for benign prostatic hypertrophy can be helpful in the morning. This is because it may help increase urine flow and reduce symptoms throughout the day. Drinking it in the morning can also help you start the day feeling more comfortable and prepared to tackle daily activities.
faq_interaction: Herbal teas like saw palmetto and stinging nettle may interact with prescription medications for benign prostatic hypertrophy. These interactions can be due to the way these herbs affect the way the body absorbs or breaks down certain medications. This may lead to changes in how well the medication works or increase the risk of side effects.
faq_pregnant: It's not recommended to consume herbal teas for benign prostatic hypertrophy while pregnant. Some herbal teas may contain caffeine or other substances that could harm the baby. These teas are usually meant for men and may not be safe for a developing fetus. It's best to stick to traditional pregnancy-friendly drinks.
faq_breastfeeding: If you're breastfeeding and have benign prostatic hypertrophy, you may be wondering about herbal teas. Some herbal teas, like saw palmetto, are thought to help with BPH symptoms. However, it's not clear if these teas are safe for breastfeeding women. If you're considering trying saw palmetto tea, it's best to check if it's in your breast milk and how it affects your baby.
